text.content:
For the GM. Three centuries ago Korvosa was raised on the Varisian coast atop a site the native Shoanti had long guarded — for buried beneath what became Castle Korvosa lie the fangs of Kazavon, a relic of a long-dead dragon-tyrant whose slow, patient evil the city never suspected.

Today the city is ruled by the aging King Eodred II, who has produced no heir — the latest in a line of rulers dogged by the so-called curse of the Crimson Throne. His young wife, Queen Ileosa, is widely written off as a beauty who married for luxury. Korvosa is about to learn how wrong that assessment is.

The hidden plot — the campaign's central secret. Ileosa found the fangs in a sealed vault beneath the castle and was infused with a shard of Kazavon's cruelty: ambition with every restraint stripped away. To seize the throne she engineered the king's death — a Red Mantis poison (fool's leprosy) fed to him through his estranged half-brother Venster, designed to mimic a disease so curative magic fails. She then murdered Venster, and when the seneschal Neolandus grew suspicious she set the Red Mantis on him — he barely survived and has gone into hiding. She is quietly allied with the Red Mantis assassins and the cult of Urgathoa. As this chapter opens, the king is days from death and the city teeters on the edge of anarchy.

The party's hook. Every PC has been wronged by the petty crime-lord Gaedren Lamm — the shared grievance the fortune-teller Zellara uses to draw them together (see @UUID[.sH9yaMZgr41grxqz]{Haunted Fortunes}). They hunt Gaedren to his lair in the Old Fishery; as they emerge with his spoils, King Eodred II dies and Korvosa erupts into the riots of A City Gone Mad.

Advancement (milestone — CHG-0007): begin at 1st level · reach 2nd after the Old Fishery · 3rd before Eel's End · 4th before the Dead Warrens · well into 4th by the chapter's end.

A harrow card finds its way to each of you — and a hand-inked message naming a man you each have cause to hate: Gaedren Lamm. The fortune-teller Zellara knows where he dens, and asks you to end him.

Zellara's reading (see @UUID[.sH9yaMZgr41grxqz]{Haunted Fortunes}) draws the party together and points them at the Old Fishery, where crime-lord Gaedren Lamm hides with his thugs, his pet crocodile @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.pathfinder-monster-core.Actor.2rMLYkUR47ZCQMUg]{Gobblegut}, and a workforce of enslaved orphans. In his den the PCs recover @UUID[Item.iNQaVcbkSM0VPXsa]{Queen Ileosa's stolen brooch}, @UUID[Item.I7HQNtjI9yPvQ4fA]{Gaedren's coded ledger}, and @UUID[Item.DPDen8gco6rznX9Y]{Zellara's haunted harrow deck} — and as they step back into the street, the king dies and Korvosa erupts into the riots of A City Gone Mad.

Advancement: the party should reach 2nd level after dealing with Gaedren. Milestone leveling (CHG-0007).

Party: 4 PCs, level 1 → 2. System: PF2e Remaster (Foundry v14 / pf2e 8.2.0).

Run it like Gotham. Tell players up front that Gaedren is a connector, not a campaign villain — this defuses the classic 'Lamm is a Lamb' let-down. By day 9 orphans toil, the thugs patrol, and the A7 doors stand open; by night every door locks, 26 orphans sleep in @UUID[.Obqhi4FG6wQ0IDZv]{A8}, and @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.pathfinder-monster-core.Actor.KHTYbQgR5hnFZdGL]{Bloo} prowls as a living alarm. The orphans are non-combatants — rescuing them is the heart of the dungeon.

The twist the players don't have yet: Zellara is already dead. Gaedren murdered her and fed her to Gobblegut; her spirit haunts her stolen deck. The PCs only learn this when they find her head in @UUID[.svCjticTit7fdDrF]{A14} — so play her in the intro as a warm, desperate ally, not a mystery.

Source: CotCT (2016 HC), Ch.1, Part 1: Haunted Fortunes — p.14-16.

It arrives where only you would find it — tucked in a spellbook, an altar, a stranger's pocket, the bottom of your tankard: a single harrow card, your card, with a few lines of bold ink on the back. It names Gaedren Lamm, and an address.

Each PC receives a harrow card matched to their highest ability + outlook, bearing an identical summons (Handout 1-1): come to 3 Lancet Street at sunset. Asking around, or a @Check[type:society|dc:12], identifies the address as the home of the Varisian fortune-teller Zellara Esmeranda.

At her home the PCs meet, compare cards, and Zellara tells her tale: Gaedren's pickpockets stole her heirloom harrow deck; her son Eran recovered it and was murdered for it; the Guard did nothing. She has tracked Gaedren to the Old Fishery at Westpier 17 and asks the PCs to bring him to justice. She then performs a harrowing to prepare them.

First Harrowing (CHG-0010). Run the reading as foreshadowing — a coming time of unrest and violence, and that the PCs are fated to become heroes of Korvosa. Mechanically, grant each PC a small pool of hero-point-style suit boons drawn from @UUID[Item.DPDen8gco6rznX9Y]{Zellara's deck}. This is the campaign's recurring subsystem; it returns at the start of every chapter.

Zellara is a haunt, not a living woman — her presence is split between this home and her stolen deck in Gaedren's lair, and her food, furnishings, and the welcome note are half-illusion (@Check[type:will|dc:18] to see through; success only makes them ghostly, not absent). If players guess early, roll with it — her purpose is simply to unite the party and aim them at Gaedren. After the fishery, a return visit finds the home long-abandoned and dust-choked; thereafter she manifests only through the deck.

No maps are shipped (copyright). Open the @UUID[Scene.PuUGEVunRqjIWFOj]{Old Fishery scene} — its map-note pins already link to each area page below, and its tokens reference the converted actors.

Drop in a battlemap (Racooze's free CotCT set, or your own at assets/maps/01-old-fishery.webp), then nudge the pins and tokens onto it — positions are staged in a grid for now (NEEDS GM REVIEW); the links are already correct. Drag @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.pathfinder-monster-core.Actor.2rMLYkUR47ZCQMUg]{Crocodile} (rename 'Gobblegut') into @UUID[.VCUD5FxhhchRv4fr]{A13} and @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.pathfinder-monster-core.Actor.KHTYbQgR5hnFZdGL]{Guard Dog} ('Bloo') into @UUID[.AdEtRIsLdlKOnWCQ]{A4}/@UUID[.Obqhi4FG6wQ0IDZv]{A8} from the pf2e Monster Core compendium.

The fishery is two map levels — an upper floor (A1–A9) and the waterline understructure (A10–A14). When you add the real map, split this into two scenes and re-pin; the area pages are already ordered for that split.

Source: CotCT (2016 HC), Ch.1, A. Old Fishery — p.17.

A creaking, mold-mottled building of boarded windows and solid old doors, perched on pilings over the Jeggare River. It still renders 'dock-dumpling' slurry from spoiled fish — a cover for the crime-lord who dens beneath it.

The fishery sits on a 13-ft embankment (@Check[type:athletics|dc:15] to climb the slick bank). Doors are hardness 5 / 10 HP (@Check[type:thievery|dc:15] to pick, or @Check[type:athletics|dc:15] to force); Yargin's brass key opens them all. Standing hazards: the @UUID[Actor.6y55S5DpXqnF40WL]{Slippery Boardwalk} (@UUID[.UNkzTXihIylWOZcn]{A3}) and the @UUID[Actor.xMhIQZS6GokTsulZ]{Rotten Ship Deck} (@UUID[.CtC18rAERxvB13di]{A9}) — either can dump a PC into @UUID[Actor.lsdWSvGJ81hDy4it]{the shark's} water (@UUID[.zXnbWKFw5vnkEuW5]{A12}).

Day vs. night. The encounters assume a daytime raid (9 orphans working, thugs at their posts). At night every door is locked, Yargin sleeps in @UUID[.HP8keHwIIY5gsNtZ]{A5}, Giggles and Hookshanks run roving patrols (≈9pm / midnight / 3am), and @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.pathfinder-monster-core.Actor.KHTYbQgR5hnFZdGL]{Bloo} wanders @UUID[.Obqhi4FG6wQ0IDZv]{A8} — his bark wakes the whole building.

The orphans ('Lamm's Lambs'). 9 work the fishery by day; all 26 sleep in @UUID[.Obqhi4FG6wQ0IDZv]{A8} at night. Rally them with @Check[type:diplomacy|dc:15] (Intimidation fails — they're inured to it); won over, they feed information or lend a hand. They know nothing past @UUID[.CtC18rAERxvB13di]{A9} — those sent below never return.

They are non-combatants and never an XP-for-kill source (CHG-0107). Invaded at night they're too frightened to fight and flee into the slums once the thugs fall. Treat a rescued orphan as a 'Missing Child' background payoff, not a stat block.

Source: CotCT (2016 HC), Ch.1, A1 — p.18.

The reek of brine and the stink of week-dead fish hang thickly in the air here. The old double doors in the side of this weathered building are tightly closed, with a drooping signpost hanging above. The sign it once displayed is long gone, leaving behind only a single short length of rusted chain.

The main doors are locked (@Check[type:thievery|dc:15]). Most business runs through @UUID[.e6d3LqdDmDo97pVA]{A7}, so a knock — or a noisy attempt on the lock — brings @UUID[Actor.7uhbgkK2IOZOlJb3]{Yargin} up from @UUID[.vejNR2kRU9hTjF7z]{A6} to answer.

Source: CotCT (2016 HC), Ch.1, A2 — p.18.

A fifteen-foot-wide loading dock abuts the side of the building here. A few carts sit nearby, partially loaded with large, tarcaked barrels marked with a fish-shaped splotch of red paint on the side. Double doors to the immediate south of the loading dock's ramp provide access to the building's interior, while a rickety flight of stairs descends ten feet to a point just three feet over the river's surface, where a simple door provides a secondary entrance.

By day the doors into @UUID[.e6d3LqdDmDo97pVA]{A7} stand open for the daily slurry shipment; the door to @UUID[.Obqhi4FG6wQ0IDZv]{A8} is always locked (@Check[type:thievery|dc:15]). The orphans do the heavy hauling under Hookshanks' eye.

Source: CotCT (2016 HC), Ch.1, A3 — p.18-20.

A slippery boardwalk clings to the side of the fishery, held together by barnacle-encrusted pilings that have had half of their thickness worn away below the waterline twelve feet below.

The @UUID[Actor.6y55S5DpXqnF40WL]{Slippery Boardwalk} hazard: a careful pace is safe, but moving fast or fighting on it risks a fall into @UUID[Actor.lsdWSvGJ81hDy4it]{the shark's} water (@UUID[.zXnbWKFw5vnkEuW5]{A12}); the planks also groan and give under heavy loads. The door into @UUID[.vejNR2kRU9hTjF7z]{A6} is locked.

Despite its state, this boardwalk is Gaedren's own private way in and out of his den (@UUID[.VCUD5FxhhchRv4fr]{A13}) — he uses it only a few times a month, spending days or weeks below to avoid being seen.

Source: CotCT (2016 HC), Ch.1, A4 — p.19.

A single desk sits in the middle of this room, with a moldy chair pushed up against it. A small pile of ratty furs and straw is heaped under the desk.

In theory Yargin meets new customers here — a rarity. Any real noise in this room quickly brings both @UUID[Actor.7uhbgkK2IOZOlJb3]{Yargin} and @UUID[Actor.qH96VshE6CZqfajv]{Hookshanks} to investigate.

  • @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.pathfinder-monster-core.Actor.KHTYbQgR5hnFZdGL]{Bloo — Guard Dog (−1)}
BlooTrivial · 20 XP

@UUID[Compendium.pf2e.pathfinder-monster-core.Actor.KHTYbQgR5hnFZdGL]{Bloo}, Yargin's foul-tempered cur, sleeps under the desk and attacks any unfamiliar scent on sight. While he lives, a thug gets a circumstance bonus to bully the orphans into fighting.

Silence him fast — @Check[type:stealth|dc:15] to slip past, @Check[type:nature|dc:15] to calm, or a quick kill — before the barking rouses the fishery.

Source: CotCT (2016 HC), Ch.1, A5 — p.20.

A pair of bunk beds sit against the far wall of this room on either side of a boarded-over window.

The thugs — Yargin, Hookshanks, and Giggles — share this room. They distrust one another and keep nothing of value here.

Source: CotCT (2016 HC), Ch.1, A6 — p.19-21.

A wooden desk sits in one corner of this room, its side preventing the western door from opening all the way. The table is heaped with dozens of slate boards covered with chalk scrawls, while to the east a cabinet slouches against the wall.

The fishery's 'books': slates of transactions and addresses, compiled monthly into scrolls in the cabinet — cover paperwork for any Guard inspection. @UUID[Actor.7uhbgkK2IOZOlJb3]{Yargin Balko} works here and carries the brass key.

A floorboard behind the chair is a hidden, barred trapdoor (@Check[type:perception|dc:18] to spot; @Check[type:athletics|dc:17] or break to force) dropping straight into the boss's chamber (@UUID[.VCUD5FxhhchRv4fr]{A13}). A meal-pulley rig makes the climb down trivial — a flanking route onto Gaedren that skips the whole lower floor.

  • @UUID[Actor.7uhbgkK2IOZOlJb3]{Yargin Balko (1)}
Yargin BalkoLow · 40 XP

Opens with thrown Acid Flasks, then falls back to his crossbow; he's terrified of melee.

Morale: at the first melee hit (or once his acid is spent) Yargin panics and flees down the trapdoor to warn Gaedren — and may lead the PCs right onto the boss. If he reaches the den, start the @UUID[.VCUD5FxhhchRv4fr]{A13} fight with Gobblegut already enraged.

Source: CotCT (2016 HC), Ch.1, A7 — p.20-22.

The stink in this room, a mixture of fish and sweat, is enough to make the eyes water. To the east, a large wooden trough holds a hideous mound of half-rancid fish, seaweed, and brine. Filthy river water and fish blood stain the floor around this trough. A pair of wooden chutes lead from this trough through holes in the eastern wall into a larger

Four orphans feed the chutes here. The floor around the trough is slick (@Check[type:acrobatics|dc:15] if moving faster than a Step). The cabinet holds petty cash, locked (@Check[type:thievery|dc:15]; Yargin's key).

@UUID[Actor.qH96VshE6CZqfajv]{Hookshanks Gruller}, a gnome taskmaster, oversees the work — and dresses as one of the orphans, passing for one until a PC beats his Deception with @Check[type:perception|dc:17] (gnome PCs get a bonus).

  • @UUID[Actor.qH96VshE6CZqfajv]{Hookshanks Gruller (1)}
  • 4× @UUID[Actor.UQr3ApF78GW7eghw]{Lamm's Lamb} (non-combatant)
Hookshanks + 4 orphansLow · 40 XP

Hookshanks orders the orphans to attack, then opens the @UUID[.AdEtRIsLdlKOnWCQ]{A4} door to loose @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.pathfinder-monster-core.Actor.KHTYbQgR5hnFZdGL]{Bloo} and raise the alarm; he fights with a kukri.

Rally the kids (@Check[type:diplomacy|dc:15]) and an older boy, Kester, blinds Hookshanks with a faceful of rancid fish. Hookshanks surrenders at low HP and tells all — except what lies in the den (@UUID[.svCjticTit7fdDrF]{A14}).

Source: CotCT (2016 HC), Ch.1, A8 — p.21-22.

The floor here is slick with river water, bits of seaweed, and fish blood, and the air is thick with the accompanying scents. Wooden catwalks to the north and south allow access between the western part of the fishery and the floor of the room, which is ten feet lower. A wide opening in the floor to the south allows direct access to the sloppy, muddy water of the Jeggare River a further three feet down, while to the northwest stands an immense eightfoot-tall wooden vat, its sides caked and waterproofed with tar. Inside is a foul-looking mixture of chum, river water, and who knows what else. To the east are stacked many barrels and crates, each with a fish painted on it. Over a dozen small hammocks hang from under the catwalks, each with its own ratty blanket and pillow.

The heart of the operation. @UUID[Actor.mHUhxeaalWkTU1h4]{Giggles}, a half-orc brute, oversees 5 orphans by day; all 26 sleep here at night. The hole drops to the river and @UUID[Actor.lsdWSvGJ81hDy4it]{the jigsaw shark} — the thugs toss scraps through it to keep the shark close and the children terrified.

  • @UUID[Actor.mHUhxeaalWkTU1h4]{Giggles (1)}
  • 5× @UUID[Actor.UQr3ApF78GW7eghw]{orphans} (non-combatant)
Giggles [+ Bloo at night]Low · 40 XP (60 with Bloo)

Giggles uses Bludgeoner to capture PCs nonlethally for Gaedren, switching to lethal below half HP and quaffing healing potions; then he fights to the death (Orc Ferocity).

Night raid: this floor is wall-to-wall sleeping children — a fight here endangers them. The river-hole is also the quiet route down to the underpier (@UUID[.zXnbWKFw5vnkEuW5]{A12}) and the den, bypassing the front rooms entirely.

Source: CotCT (2016 HC), Ch.1, A9 — p.23-24.

The rotten deck of this ancient sailing ship seems to be barely intact; its hull is worn and thick with seaweed and barnacles. The ship is held together primarily by the layers of old rope that lash it securely to the pilings that support the fishery and the nearby boardwalk. The rickety walkway leads along the ship's starboard, a foot below its railing. A single wooden door leading into the aft cabin bears a crude painting of a red fish on its surface.

No longer seaworthy — Gaedren's route to the den. The @UUID[Actor.xMhIQZS6GokTsulZ]{Rotten Ship Deck} hazard: everything but the stern is rotten, and a Medium+ creature moving toward the bow crashes through into the hold (@UUID[.yz5Ao0tTfBxREHWy]{A11}). Stairs in the cabin lead down to the spider nest.

Source: CotCT (2016 HC), Ch.1, A10 — p.24.

The air in this room is thick and musty. Thick sheets of cobwebs hang from the walls and mounds of blankets, cushions, and straw clutter the floor. To the south, a narrow flight of stairs leads down into the ship's hold.

The cabin is the lair of a single cat-sized @UUID[Actor.1M94tm3wnSRGcTG5]{drain spider} that lunges at the first creature through the door.

  • @UUID[Actor.1M94tm3wnSRGcTG5]{Drain Spider (−1)}
Drain SpiderTrivial · 20 XP

One drain spider, off-guard against the surprised intruder it lunges at.

The spiders can't abide the vermin repellent looted from @UUID[Item.ymuRwMg6WfWIwkne]{Gaedren's coffer} (@UUID[.VCUD5FxhhchRv4fr]{A13}) — a coated creature auto-repels them.

Source: CotCT (2016 HC), Ch.1, A11 — p.24.

Dark and dank, the ship's hold smells of mildew. Several barrels, crates, and other containers lie stacked here and there. Dust and grime cover the floor, except where river water has collected in puddles.

Four more @UUID[Actor.1M94tm3wnSRGcTG5]{drain spiders} infest the hold.

Gaedren built a secret door into the hull (@Check[type:perception|dc:17]) opening to the underpier (@UUID[.zXnbWKFw5vnkEuW5]{A12}) and the boss. Spotting his tracks in the filth (@Check[type:survival|dc:15]) grants a circumstance bonus to find it.

  • 4× @UUID[Actor.1M94tm3wnSRGcTG5]{Drain Spider (−1)}
Drain Spider nestModerate · 80 XP (trivial with repellent)

Four drain spiders swarm anything that enters. Pair this with the @UUID[Actor.xMhIQZS6GokTsulZ]{Rotten Deck} collapse from @UUID[.CtC18rAERxvB13di]{A9} for a single nasty moment.

Source: CotCT (2016 HC), Ch.1, A12 — p.24-25.

A narrow space exists under the fishery, with about three feet of room between the floor of the eastern side of the building and the languid, foamy river water below. Wooden pilings support the building, and thick mats of moss and cobwebs hang from ropes and rusted chains between them. A wooden walkway floats on the river's surface, winding along the inner wall of pilings that supports the building's frame above. The walkway leads from the sodden ship to the east all the way west to a small two-and-a-half-foot-square door that leads into an understructure below the fishery's land-bound half. The pilings below this understructure are densely arrayed, leaving only narrow gaps into the water below that area.

Gaedren's escape skiffs are tied along the walkway. The squat door (@Check[type:thievery|dc:17]) leads into the den (@UUID[.VCUD5FxhhchRv4fr]{A13}); a Medium creature must stoop to squeeze through.

  • @UUID[Actor.lsdWSvGJ81hDy4it]{Jigsaw Shark (1)}
Jigsaw SharkLow · 40 XP · avoidable

The shark scavenges scraps drifting from above. It attacks anything that falls into the water, but only leaps onto the walkway if it is first attacked and damaged.

Stay dry and it stays a hazard, not a fight — though the @UUID[Actor.6y55S5DpXqnF40WL]{boardwalk} and @UUID[Actor.xMhIQZS6GokTsulZ]{rotten deck} above exist precisely to feed it victims.

Source: CotCT (2016 HC), Ch.1, A13 — p.24-26.

The air in this large room is chilly and stinks of the river, thanks to a huge opening in the floor that drops away to the river shore three feet below. Several pilings emerge from the waters to support the roof eight feet above the floor, with mossy ropes slung between them. In two places, rusty manacles hang from the ropes over the water. Two five-foot-wide walkways cross the hole to the other side of the chamber, where a collection of old cabinets, lockboxes, and piles of clutter are strewn about. Chipped porcelain plates, a cracked goblet, badly rusted silverware, an old wooden shield with a crossbow bolt embedded in it, the odd dinged helm, and other 'treasures' litter the floor of this side of the chamber. Three tables heaped with clutter stand amid this mess. In the southwest corner, a wooden door provides access to a walled-off section.

@UUID[Compendium.pf2e.pathfinder-monster-core.Actor.2rMLYkUR47ZCQMUg]{Gobblegut} lurks in the pit; @UUID[Actor.RKfT6vJ5guinSBjo]{Gaedren Lamm} sorts the day's haul at his tables across it. Reaching him means crossing the gator's water.

Those manacles are where Gaedren feeds doomed orphans to Gobblegut for sport — the PCs may arrive mid-'feeding,' a child dangling over the snapping jaws. Anyone who names Zellara earns a leering reply that 'she's in the next room' — her head waits in @UUID[.svCjticTit7fdDrF]{A14}. Tactics: Gaedren opens with Spur the Beast to enrage Gobblegut, snipes from across the pool, Nimble Dodges focus fire, and flees to the @UUID[.zXnbWKFw5vnkEuW5]{A12} skiffs at ≤8 HP — though the abused gator may take him first.

  • @UUID[Actor.RKfT6vJ5guinSBjo]{Gaedren Lamm (2)}
  • @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.pathfinder-monster-core.Actor.2rMLYkUR47ZCQMUg]{Gobblegut — Crocodile (2)}
BOSS — Gaedren + GobblegutSEVERE · 120 XP @ level 1 (Moderate @ level 2)

The crocodile is the real threat; Gaedren is a frail catalyst who weaponizes it and snipes from cover.

Scaling: 3 PCs → Weak Crocodile · 5–6 PCs → Elite Crocodile and/or pull a drain spider from the pool · if Yargin reached Gaedren first, Gobblegut starts pre-enraged.

On the tables: @UUID[Item.ymuRwMg6WfWIwkne]{a darkwood coffer — 20 doses of shiver + 7 applications of vermin repellent}.

⚠ Track what the PCs do with the body. Left here, Gaedren's son Rolth animates it — the PCs meet an undead Gaedren in the Dead Warrens at the chapter's end. See @UUID[.CEuUTWkaC01YQDo5]{Conversion Notes}.

Source: CotCT (2016 HC), Ch.1, A14 — p.26-28.

This foul-smelling room seems to be a combination bedroom and study. A wooden bed with a lumpy mattress stands against the east wall, while a round table heaped with dirty plates, bread crusts, stained goblets, fruit rinds, and scuttling cockroaches sits nearby. At the foot of the bed sits a large strongbox; a slightly rusted lock secures it and a moldy ledger with pages rippled from moisture sits atop its lid. A sagging dresser filled with moth-eaten clothes well past their glory days is in one corner. What appears to be a wooden hatbox surrounded by a small cloud of flies sits atop this dresser.

Gaedren's private quarters. The strongbox opens to the rusty iron key he carries, or @Check[type:thievery|dc:15].

The reveal. The hatbox holds Zellara's severed head, crudely made up to mimic life (@Check[type:medicine|dc:14]: dead for weeks) — confirming the woman who hired the party has been dead all along. Beneath it lies @UUID[Item.DPDen8gco6rznX9Y]{her haunted harrow deck}, still inhabited by her spirit. Atop the strongbox is @UUID[Item.I7HQNtjI9yPvQ4fA]{Gaedren's coded ledger}; inside, @UUID[Item.iNQaVcbkSM0VPXsa]{Queen Ileosa's Brooch} and the hoard. A key-shaped masterwork dagger among the loot is a gift from Gaedren's estranged son Rolth — foreshadowing the next chapter (@Check[type:society|dc:20] recognizes the killer's signature blade).

Full parcel on the @UUID[.tkc0z28kYBKNBgHd]{Treasure} page.

Click any creature to open its sheet. Build rationale: reports/npc_monster_conversion_report.md.

  • @UUID[Actor.RKfT6vJ5guinSBjo]{Gaedren Lamm} (L2) — @UUID[.VCUD5FxhhchRv4fr]{A13} boss · @UUID[Actor.7uhbgkK2IOZOlJb3]{Yargin Balko} (L1) — @UUID[.vejNR2kRU9hTjF7z]{A6} · @UUID[Actor.qH96VshE6CZqfajv]{Hookshanks Gruller} (L1) — @UUID[.e6d3LqdDmDo97pVA]{A7} · @UUID[Actor.mHUhxeaalWkTU1h4]{Giggles} (L1) — @UUID[.Obqhi4FG6wQ0IDZv]{A8}
  • @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.pathfinder-monster-core.Actor.2rMLYkUR47ZCQMUg]{Gobblegut} = Crocodile (L2) · @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.pathfinder-monster-core.Actor.KHTYbQgR5hnFZdGL]{Bloo} = Guard Dog (L−1) · @UUID[Actor.1M94tm3wnSRGcTG5]{Drain Spider} (L−1) · @UUID[Actor.lsdWSvGJ81hDy4it]{Jigsaw Shark} (L1) · @UUID[Actor.UQr3ApF78GW7eghw]{Lamm's Lambs} (non-combatant)
  • Zellara — the harrow-spirit guide, bound to @UUID[Item.DPDen8gco6rznX9Y]{her deck} (story NPC, no stat block).

Rebuilt to PF2e level 1→2 (the PF1e gp hoard is discarded; CHG-0008). Full table: reports/treasure_report.md.

  • @UUID[Item.b04PLWVkmm3RM4nm]{+1 Dagger (Gaedren's)} — boss loot, a fitting first permanent magic weapon · +1 armor potency (his padded armor) · A14 hoard → a 1st-rank scroll + ~50 gp gems.
  • @UUID[Item.dK4hnpH7s67RYnNI]{Garnet amulet} (~8 gp) · @UUID[Item.ymuRwMg6WfWIwkne]{Coffer + vermin repellent} (~12 gp) · 2× Acid Flask + Thunderstone (Yargin) · 3× minor Healing Potion (Giggles) · ~30 gp coin.

Story items — do not let players cash these out for level-breaking gold. @UUID[Item.iNQaVcbkSM0VPXsa]{Queen Ileosa's Brooch} launches the queen's audience and the chapter's main reward; @UUID[Item.DPDen8gco6rznX9Y]{Zellara's Harrow Deck} is the Harrow subsystem anchor (CHG-0010). The original PF1e parcel (a 2,000 gp brooch, gold ingot, etc.) is deliberately not restored.

Mirrors conversion_change_log.md (CHG-0101…0108). Story, villain, flow, and the closing cliffhanger are all preserved; nothing here is an approval-gated change.

  • Gaedren rebuilt as a credible Severe, with Gobblegut as the real threat — fixes the 'Lamm is a Lamb' anticlimax and PF2e solo-boss math. Added Spur the Beast / Nimble Dodge; kept Limping Gait. ⚠ Confirm Severe @L1 vs running it @L2.
  • Gobblegut & Bloo referenced as the official Crocodile / Guard Dog (no rebuild).
  • DCs re-derived to PF2e level-based / simple values (the PF1e locked-door spam collapsed to a single 'pick = 15' line).
  • Treasure rebuilt to PF2e level 1→2 (CHG-0008).
  • Drain Spider Venom: PF1e Con-drain → PF2e 1-stage poison + enfeebled.
  • Harrowing → hero-point-style suit pool (CHG-0010).
  • Orphans = explicit non-combatants (Rally / Cower); never an XP-for-kill source (CHG-0107).
  • Forward hooks: leaving Gaedren's body → undead Gaedren in the Dead Warrens; the Brooch → the queen's audience; the deck → recurring Harrowings.
